title: Model Lab Adaptive Clustering, Heatmap Fusion, and Cluster Intelligence Panel

clustering_heatmap_strategy.countywide: At countywide scale, the Development Research Signal uses fused aggregate surface cells instead of drawing all individual parcel-safe markers.
clustering_heatmap_strategy.medium_zoom: At medium scale, the overlay renders count-scaled cluster markers. Cluster color reflects the dominant safe research band and marker size reflects capped feature count.
clustering_heatmap_strategy.close_zoom: At close scale, the overlay switches to capped individual parcel-safe research markers using relative research band colors.

area_naming_logic.implemented: Uses honest fallback labels derived from cluster contents, such as Research cluster of N parcels or Research surface cell of N parcels.
area_naming_logic.not_implemented: No corridor, municipality, subdivision, or place names are invented when source context is not available in the preview marker payload.

click_behavior.cluster_click: Cluster and heat-surface graphics carry safe aggregate fields so the right panel can show Selected Research Cluster, count, dominant band, band distribution, top drivers, display mode, and caveat.
click_behavior.marker_click: Parcel-detail markers continue to show Selected Development Research Context with parcel-safe identifier, research band, relative rank band, top drivers, data quality flag, and caveat.
click_behavior.fallback: Added a nearest rendered model-research graphic fallback for map clicks when ArcGIS hitTest misses a nearby safe cluster or marker.

right_panel_changes.default_state: Model Lab Intelligence stays concise and shows current display mode, overlay status, map-reading guidance, Explain the Numbers, and collapsed Model QA details.
right_panel_changes.selected_cluster_state: Selected Research Cluster shows area label, cluster count, dominant band, band distribution, top drivers, why highlighted, map scale/display mode, and internal-only caveat.
right_panel_changes.selected_marker_state: Selected Development Research Context remains parcel-safe and avoids exact probabilities, raw scores, and official prediction classes.

performance_improvements: Avoids rendering all individual research markers at countywide scale.
Aggregates markers into countywide cells and medium-zoom clusters.
Caps rendered aggregate cells and parcel-detail markers.
Memoizes derived display-mode and aggregation data.
Reduces symbol halo sizes and opacity to lower visual clutter.

snapshot_report_integration.report_behavior: Planning Snapshot / Executive Summary now includes cluster or heatmap-cell context when selected, including Cluster / cell count and Band distribution, while preserving the required no-probability caveat.

safety_verification.safety_audit_notes: Unsafe tokens found by rg are limited to existing internal API/schema/service guardrail fields and safe hook checks such as prediction_probability_available=false, exact_probability_available=false, no_raw_model_scores, and backend mappings that translate internal classes into safe research labels. Phase 23G user-facing sources were covered by tests to reject unsafe public wording.

smoke_test_results.countywide_fused_view_text: passed: UI showed Countywide research surface and Warmer areas guidance
smoke_test_results.unsafe_terms_in_browser_ui: passed: none found for exact/raw/probability terms checked
smoke_test_results.cluster_click_panel_update: not conclusively browser-verified; bounded automated clicks in the small SceneView canvas missed a selectable rendered research feature, but cluster context fields, selected-panel UI, nearest-click fallback, and report integration are covered by static tests
smoke_test_results.browser_console_errors: not fully rechecked after the final click attempt; earlier smoke showed no unexpected app errors beyond known ArcGIS external behavior

remaining_limitations: Cluster area labels intentionally use honest fallback wording until safe road, jurisdiction, or neighborhood context is available in the preview payload.
Automated browser smoke did not conclusively select a cluster because canvas clicks missed rendered research graphics at the tested viewport.
No backend clustering endpoint was added; Phase 23G uses frontend aggregation over the existing safe research preview markers.
